events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes aluminum conductors on a device not rated CO/ALR. This is a non-compliant condition. Aluminum conductors must be terminated on devices rated CO/ALR (Copper/Aluminum Revised) per NEC requirements. This is a fire hazard due to aluminum's oxidation and thermal expansion characteristics.

Decision: fail
Risk: high (fire hazard)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: ~80 (rough-complete)
Value: 0 (no numeric reading requested beyond the condition assessment)
Confidence: high

Findings: Aluminum conductor terminated on device not rated CO/ALR - non-compliant and fire hazard.
Actions: Replace device with CO/ALR-rated device or use copper pigtails with antioxidant compound.
References: NEC 310, Table 310.16, NEC 404.14(C) for CO/ALR requirements.
